details.heading: Your transaction is secure
details.content: We work hard to protect your security and privacy. Our payment security system encrypts your information during transmission. We don’t share your credit card details with third-party sellers, and we don’t sell your information to others. Learn more

about_item: 【Enclosed Design with Ventilation Holes】: Unlike traditional crates with metal bars, this innovative design features sturdy panels with strategically placed small holes (diameter 0.5 inch). These ventilation holes ensure ample airflow while creating a den-like and enclosed space that makes your dog feel secure, helping alleviate anxiety.

【Removable Floor for Easy Cleaning】: The floor is removable for quick cleaning, keeping your pet's space hygiene without hard-to-reach corners. Unlike wire floors, this metal bottom panel provides stable, comfortable support for your dog’s paws and is more resistant to damage, extending the crate’s life.
【Double Doors and Escape Proof Locks】: The double doors feature allows for easier entry and exit, while the upgraded lock design offers enhanced security, ensuring your escape artist stay safe.
【Sturdy Materials and Long-Lasting Durability】: This heavy duty dog crate is made of high-quality steel and reinforced particleboard, preventing the large kennel from being damaged or rusting.

【One Year Warranty】: We take responsibility for any quality problem and commit to providing top-notch customer service. If there are any quality problems within one year from the date of purchase, please contact us for a replacement or refund.

summary.text: Customers praise the dog crate's top-notch workmanship and solid construction, with one noting it hasn't dented or bent. The crate is easy to assemble with clear instructions, and customers find it durable, with one mentioning their dog hasn't broken out of it. They appreciate its appearance, with one noting it looks less like a dog bed, and its size, accommodating two full-grown 50-lb Border Collies. Customers value its safety features, keeping pets secure and helping them feel safe, though opinions about the latches' security are mixed.

authors_reviews.title: WOW!! My Houdini Can’t Escape
authors_reviews.text: I have a Weimaraner that loves to tear up his metal wire kennels. This kennel is not only durable, but it is extremely stable. It is made from quality materials and provides my dog comfort while he’s in there. The locking mechanism are easy to use and the kennel is easily accessible from the sides and top.
authors_reviews.author: Hamilton

authors_reviews.title: Belgian Malinois proof !!
authors_reviews.text: This kennel is belgian malinois proof! [Picture of his first kennel below] It didn't arrive later than expected but seller was very attentive and professional, gave a $10 refund for it not arriving on time. Kennel is well made. Probably should have got a smaller one. He is about 48-50 lbs and had lots of room to move around. If he pees in the kennel is does leak to the floor. Maybe if should be one solid bottom instead of 2. Overall, no complaints, definitely worth the money.
authors_reviews.author: Betsy Folse

authors_reviews.title: So comfy, they couldn’t wait!
authors_reviews.text: Pups loved it so much they couldn’t wait for me to finish putting it together before they climbed in!Came packaged really well. VERY HEAVY. The instructions say it requires 2 adults, but that’s really only when you need to flip it over. The instructions were clear and the assembly was pretty straightforward.The panels are sturdy and the latches seem to have been well designed. Ours are still puppies, so we haven’t tested them against a big dog yet. Our pups love sleeping in here at night and I sleep better knowing they’re not chewing up my house. 😁Also love the tray underneath for puppy pads - generally a really well designed kennel. I’m terrible with dimensions, but was pleased that it was even bigger than I expected. I bought 2 but won’t assemble the second one until they’re bigger.
authors_reviews.author: Jill Hudson

authors_reviews.title: Perfect for big dogs with anxiety!
authors_reviews.text: Our new Rottweiler puppy (9 months) developed some serious separation anxiety once he bonded with us. He is 65 pounds currently. His wire crate he broke out of multiple times and busted up his nose. He chews so he can’t stay out. This crate is AMAZING. Not to mention it is much nicer to look at. We have had zero issues with it. He isn’t happy in there but he cannot harm himself or break out. We have used it for 3 months now and it’s still great. The only negative is the latches. They suck. Other reviews said they ratchet strap it shut but I’m not doing that. I put camping stakes under the latch (see photo) and it stays shut just fine.
authors_reviews.author: Andrea

authors_reviews.title: Worth the money for large dogs. Plenty of room. Sturdy.
authors_reviews.text: Perfect fit for 60lb shelter mutt that struggled with separation anxiety and did NOT like the fireworks.The front doors are very secure. The smaller holes keep it dark, I have a music box playing for my dog and he is so much less reactive during the day. The tray underneath is so much more secure and easy to work with than on wire crates. The slats are far enough apart the kibble can get through rather than rot, but close enough together it does not hurt their feet.The wheels are awesome and the overall construction is incredibly sturdy. The top door is great for feeding, refilling water, or training. The latches are also insanely secure.I looked at this exact crate before bringing my dog home and I wish I didn't waste $115 on a wire crates and just bought this.
authors_reviews.author: Blaine Lewis

authors_reviews.title: Heavy duty dog crate
authors_reviews.text: Well made and truly a furniture piece. We have another crate we bought but cost way more. I think the value for your money is excellent!
authors_reviews.author: KFenbert

authors_reviews.title: It's ok
authors_reviews.text: It's definitely more "heavy duty" than the standard wire crates. Most of that comes from the weight from the floor panels which are the sturdiest part of the kennel. If the kennel was made from the metal panels the floor is made out of, it would be truly heavy duty. If your dog tries to escape by digging, that floor panel should stop them. The side/front/back panels with the holes are wobbly, thin metal that flex with mild pressure. Our 35+ lb dog scratched to the bare metal after one night in it. I'm not sure who thought that putting panels of MDF inside the kennel was a good idea in a "chew proof" cage. I thought it was just decorative on the outside and covered by metal on the inside, but that's not the case. The slider locks are as thin or thinner than standard wire cage sliders and held in place by the tiniest screws I've ever seen, but there are four of them that latch. I imagine that a large dog that was committed to escaping could bend them out of shape. There is quite a gap between the doors and the stop when they are locked so if your dog bounces off of them, it sounds like someone is banging a hammer on a metal trash can. We put some felt buttons that you put under furniture between the door and the stop to keep it from rattling. It isn't perfect, but it is one of the few kennels that is more durable than a wire cage but not crazy expensive. I have a hard time believing reviewers saying this is "specific dog breed proof" and then name a monster-sized dog. Our little terrier mix beat it up pretty bad in one night. If you have a larger, more powerful, or crazier dog than we do, I would spend the money on a real chew/escape proof cage. It would be worth the extra money - about 3-4x more expensive than this one. If you just need something sturdier than a normal wire crate, this should work fine.
authors_reviews.author: J. Koon

authors_reviews.title: Sturdy and early delivery
authors_reviews.text: It came early and was just as described. Very sturdy and easy to put together
authors_reviews.author: Jeanette Lyons
